text.title: MVP Scoping Examples and Antipatterns
text.summary: Time-tracking app and course platform worked examples with MoSCoW scope decisions and learning goals, plus antipatterns.

example.note: Problem: freelancers forget to track time and lose billable hours. Starting feature dump: 40+ features including mobile app, team features, integrations, advanced reports, budgets, etc.

MVP scope (4 weeks): Must Have — start/stop timer (core functionality), project selection (assign time), basic report (see tracked time), simple invoice (get paid). Won't Have: mobile app (responsive web suffices), team features (solo focus), integrations (manual for now), advanced reports (spreadsheet export).
Learning goal: will users actually track time daily? Kill signal: if fewer than 40% of registered users log time on 3+ days in the first 2 weeks, validate a different approach.

Problem: course creators struggle with tech setup. MVP scope (6 weeks): Must Have — upload video/text lessons (core content), paywall/payment (must monetize), student access control (protect content), basic progress tracking (show value).
Won't Have: quizzes (manual feedback), certificates (v2), community features (use external Discord), mobile app (responsive web), advanced analytics (basic counts only).
Learning goal: will creators pay $29/month for simplicity over full-featured competitors? Kill signal: if 0 of 20 early-access invitees pay after trial, pivot pricing or positioning.

antipattern.reason: Every item added to Must-Have extends the timeline and narrows the learning. With 10 Must-Haves, the MVP is a 3-month project. Cap at 5 and demote the rest.
Without explicit exclusions, scope creep enters through "just one more thing" requests. The Won't-Have list is a public commitment that prevents renegotiation of every cut feature.

antipattern.description: No learning goal or kill signal.
antipattern.reason: An MVP without a hypothesis is just a small product launch. Without a pre-agreed failure criterion, teams ship regardless of results and call it validated.
If it takes 6 months, it is not minimum. Revisit scope, cut Must-Haves further, or use micro-MVPs to validate assumptions before building.

antipattern.description: Building MVP without weekly user feedback.
antipattern.reason: Assumptions invalidated at week 2 can redirect the remaining build. Waiting until launch to discover the hypothesis is wrong wastes all remaining build time.
